subject: Chemistry
chapter: Haloalkanes and Haloarenes; Alcohols, Phenols and Ethers

questions.question: The IUPAC name of CH3CH2OH is ______.
questions.explanation: CH3CH2OH is a two-carbon alcohol. Its IUPAC name is ethanol.

questions.question: An alcohol in which the hydroxyl-bearing carbon is attached to only one other carbon atom is classified as a ______.
questions.explanation: A primary alcohol has the carbon bearing the OH group attached to only one other carbon atom.

questions.question: The IUPAC name of CH3OCH2CH3 is ______.
questions.explanation: Ethers are named as alkoxyalkanes. CH3OCH2CH3 is methoxyethane.

questions.question: The reagent commonly used to oxidise a primary alcohol into an aldehyde without further oxidation is ______.
questions.explanation: Cro3, PCC oxidises primary alcohols to aldehydes without significant further oxidation.

questions.question: The reaction in which an alcohol reacts with a carboxylic acid to form an ester is called ______.
questions.explanation: Alcohols react with carboxylic acids in the presence of acid catalyst to give esters.

questions.question: The common laboratory test used to distinguish primary, secondary and tertiary alcohols using concentrated HCl and anhydrous ZnCl2 is the ______.
questions.explanation: Lucas test distinguishes alcohols based on the rate of formation of alkyl chlorides.

questions.question: Heating ethanol vapours over copper at 573 K produces the aldehyde ______.
questions.explanation: Primary alcohols are dehydrogenated by hot copper at 573 K to give aldehydes.

questions.question: The product formed in the Wurtz reaction of two molecules of ethyl bromide is ______.
questions.explanation: In the Wurtz reaction, two ethyl groups couple to form butane.

questions.question: The product formed in the Sandmeyer reaction when benzene diazonium chloride is treated with CuCl is ______.
questions.explanation: In the Sandmeyer reaction, benzene diazonium chloride reacts with CuCl to form chlorobenzene.

questions.question: The named reaction used to convert an alkyl chloride or bromide into an alkyl fluoride is the ______.
questions.explanation: The Swarts reaction prepares alkyl fluorides from alkyl chlorides or bromides.

questions.question: The reagent used for converting alcohols into alkyl bromides under mild conditions is ______.
questions.explanation: Phosphorus tribromide (PBr3) converts primary and secondary alcohols into alkyl bromides efficiently.

questions.question: The intermolecular force mainly responsible for the unusually high boiling points of alcohols is ______.
questions.explanation: Alcohol molecules are associated through intermolecular hydrogen bonding, increasing their boiling points.

questions.question: The product obtained when propan-2-ol is oxidised is ______.
questions.explanation: Oxidation of a secondary alcohol produces a ketone. Propan-2-ol gives propanone.

questions.question: The major alkene formed on dehydration of propan-2-ol is ______.
questions.explanation: On dehydration with concentrated H2SO4 and heat, propan-2-ol undergoes elimination of water to form propene.

questions.question: The major alkene formed during dehydration generally follows the ______.
questions.explanation: According to Zaitsev's rule, the more substituted alkene is usually the major product.

questions.question: The common name of o-hydroxybenzoic acid is ______.
questions.explanation: o-Hydroxybenzoic acid is commonly known as salicylic acid.

questions.question: The IUPAC name of CH3CH2Cl is ______.
questions.explanation: CH3CH2Cl is the two-carbon haloalkane named chloroethane.

questions.question: The conversion of an alkyl chloride or bromide into an alkyl iodide using NaI in dry acetone is called the ______.
questions.explanation: The Finkelstein reaction replaces chlorine or bromine with iodine using sodium iodide in dry acetone.

questions.question: The conversion of a benzene diazonium salt into chlorobenzene using CuCl is called the ______.
questions.explanation: The Sandmeyer reaction converts aryl diazonium salts into haloarenes using cuprous salts.

questions.question: In the reaction 3R–OH + PBr3 → 3R–Br + H3PO3, PBr3 is prepared in situ using red phosphorus and ______.
questions.explanation: In the laboratory, phosphorus tribromide is prepared in situ by reacting red phosphorus with bromine (Br2). The PBr3 formed then converts alcohols into alkyl bromides according to: 3R–OH + PBr3 → 3R–Br + H3PO3.

questions.question: Why is phenol more acidic than ethanol?
options: Ethanol contains a stronger C–O bond
Phenol has weaker O–H bond because of hydrogen bonding
questions.explanation: The phenoxide ion formed after loss of H+ is resonance stabilised, whereas the ethoxide ion is not.

questions.question: Arrange the following in decreasing order of boiling point: Ethanol, Dimethyl ether, Ethane.
questions.explanation: Alcohols exhibit hydrogen bonding, ethers have dipole-dipole interactions and hydrocarbons possess only dispersion forces.

questions.question: Why is tert-butyl alcohol resistant to oxidation under ordinary oxidising conditions?
options: It has no α-hydrogen attached to the carbon bearing the OH group
It is insoluble in oxidising agents
questions.explanation: Tertiary alcohols lack an α-hydrogen, making oxidation difficult without carbon-carbon bond cleavage.

questions.question: Which reagent converts a primary alcohol into an aldehyde without significant further oxidation?
questions.explanation: PCC selectively oxidises primary alcohols to aldehydes.

questions.question: Arrange the following in increasing acidity: Ethanol, Water, Phenol.
questions.explanation: Phenol is most acidic due to resonance stabilisation. Water is more acidic than ethanol.

questions.question: Why does hydroboration-oxidation of propene mainly produce propan-1-ol?
options: It removes the double bond before oxidation
questions.explanation: Hydroboration-oxidation proceeds through anti-Markovnikov addition, placing the OH group on the less substituted carbon.

questions.question: Which alcohol gives a ketone on passing its vapours over heated copper at 573 K?
questions.explanation: Secondary alcohols undergo dehydrogenation to ketones over heated copper.

questions.question: Arrange the following in decreasing boiling point: Propan-1-ol, Propan-2-ol, 2-Methylpropan-2-ol.
questions.explanation: Greater branching reduces surface area and intermolecular attraction, lowering boiling point.

questions.question: Which statement correctly explains why alcohols are generally more soluble in water than ethers of similar molecular mass?
options: Alcohols form hydrogen bonds with water through their O–H group
Ethers have stronger hydrogen bonding than alcohols
questions.explanation: The hydroxyl group of alcohols forms strong hydrogen bonds with water, increasing solubility.

questions.question: Which reagent is used in the Lucas test?
questions.explanation: Lucas reagent consists of concentrated hydrochloric acid and anhydrous zinc chloride.

questions.question: Why does phenol react with aqueous NaOH whereas ethanol does not?
options: Phenol is sufficiently acidic to form sodium phenoxide
Phenol is a stronger base than ethanol
questions.explanation: Phenol is more acidic due to resonance stabilisation of the phenoxide ion and therefore reacts with NaOH.

questions.question: Arrange the following in decreasing acidity: p-Nitrophenol, Phenol, p-Cresol.
questions.explanation: The electron-withdrawing nitro group increases acidity, whereas the methyl group decreases it.

questions.question: Why is dry ether used during the preparation of Grignard reagents?
options: It prevents hydrolysis by excluding moisture and stabilises the reagent
It converts magnesium into Mg²⁺ ions
questions.explanation: Grignard reagents are destroyed by water. Dry ether provides an anhydrous medium and stabilises the reagent.

questions.question: Which alcohol is produced when methylmagnesium bromide reacts with methanal followed by hydrolysis?
questions.explanation: Reaction with methanal always gives a primary alcohol containing one additional carbon atom.

questions.question: Arrange the following in decreasing solubility in water: Methanol, Ethanol, Propan-1-ol.
questions.explanation: As alkyl chain length increases, the hydrophobic effect increases and water solubility decreases.

questions.question: Why does concentrated H₂SO₄ promote dehydration of alcohols?
options: It behaves as a reducing agent
It protonates the hydroxyl group, converting it into a better leaving group
It removes oxygen from the alcohol
It acts only as a catalyst for oxidation
It converts alcohols into ethers only
questions.explanation: Protonation converts –OH into H₂O, a much better leaving group, facilitating elimination.

questions.question: Which product is obtained when phenol is acetylated using acetic anhydride?
questions.explanation: Acetylation introduces an acetyl group on oxygen, producing phenyl acetate.

questions.question: Arrange the following in decreasing reactivity towards Lucas reagent: tert-Butyl alcohol, Propan-2-ol, Ethanol.
questions.explanation: Lucas reagent reacts fastest with tertiary alcohols, followed by secondary and then primary alcohols.

questions.question: Why is sodium metal able to liberate hydrogen from alcohols?
options: The O–H hydrogen is weakly acidic and is displaced by sodium
questions.explanation: The hydroxyl hydrogen is displaced by sodium to form sodium alkoxide and hydrogen gas.

questions.question: Which alcohol gives no oxidation product with PCC, KMnO₄ or CrO₃ under ordinary conditions?
questions.explanation: Tertiary alcohols lack an α-hydrogen and therefore resist oxidation under ordinary conditions.

questions.question: Why does phenol undergo electrophilic substitution more readily than benzene?
options: The –OH group withdraws electrons by resonance
The –OH group donates electron density to the ring by resonance
The –OH group deactivates the ring completely
questions.explanation: The lone pair on oxygen is delocalised into the aromatic ring by resonance, increasing electron density and activating the ring toward electrophilic substitution.

questions.question: Which reagent is used in the Kolbe reaction for preparing salicylic acid from sodium phenoxide?
questions.explanation: Carbon dioxide reacts with sodium phenoxide under pressure in the Kolbe reaction to form sodium salicylate, which on acidification gives salicylic acid.

questions.question: Arrange the following in decreasing ease of dehydration: tert-Butyl alcohol, Propan-2-ol, Ethanol.
questions.explanation: Dehydration proceeds through carbocation formation. Tertiary alcohols dehydrate most readily, followed by secondary and then primary alcohols.

questions.question: Why does dehydration of alcohols generally produce the more substituted alkene as the major product?
options: The less substituted alkene is always less stable
The double bond always forms at carbon-1
Hydrogen is removed from the α-carbon only
Alcohols undergo substitution instead of elimination
questions.explanation: According to Zaitsev's rule, the more substituted and more stable alkene is generally the major product.

questions.question: Which alcohol is obtained when ethylmagnesium bromide reacts with ethanal followed by hydrolysis?
questions.explanation: A Grignard reagent reacts with an aldehyde (other than methanal) to produce a secondary alcohol after hydrolysis.

questions.question: Why must Grignard reagents never be prepared in the presence of water?
options: Water converts the reagent into an alkene
Water destroys the Grignard reagent by protonating it
questions.explanation: Grignard reagents are extremely basic and react immediately with water to give hydrocarbons, destroying the reagent.

questions.question: Which reagent converts phenol into phenyl acetate?
questions.explanation: Phenol undergoes acetylation with acetic anhydride (or acetyl chloride) to form phenyl acetate.

questions.question: Arrange the following in decreasing boiling point: Methanol, Ethanol, Propan-1-ol.
questions.explanation: Boiling point increases with molecular mass when hydrogen bonding is similar, giving Propan-1-ol > Ethanol > Methanol.

questions.question: Why does phenol not give the Lucas test under ordinary conditions?
options: The C–O bond in phenol has partial double-bond character due to resonance
questions.explanation: The aryl C–O bond in phenol has partial double-bond character because of resonance and does not undergo the substitution required for the Lucas test.

questions.question: Which reagent is preferred for converting alcohols into alkyl chlorides because its gaseous by-products escape from the reaction mixture?
questions.explanation: Thionyl chloride (SOCl₂) produces gaseous SO₂ and HCl as by-products, leaving relatively pure alkyl chloride.
